purpose: Measure the size and direction of adjusted racial gaps in judge-level criminal sentencing patterns without making findings about motive, misconduct, or individual cases.

analysis_window.type: shared rolling window per city release
analysis_window.end_date_rule: Latest verified sentence date in the city release unless an approved release date is supplied

outcomes: Length of incarceration sentence among cases with time to serve
comparison_rule: Each supported racial group is compared separately with White defendants. No combined minority group is used as the primary comparison.
estimator: Deterministic additive control adjustment with group-specific residual comparisons and a seeded bootstrap uncertainty interval.
score_direction: 0 means a smaller measured racial gap; 100 means a bigger measured racial gap.
overall_score_rule: The overall Disparity Score is the larger of the incarceration and sentence-length component scores so a larger signal is not averaged away.
limitations: Synthetic validation does not establish validity on any real court system.
The model measures associations in available records and does not establish causation, intent, racism, discrimination, or misconduct.
Unmeasured case facts, source-system limitations, charging decisions, plea negotiations, and statutory constraints may affect observed sentencing patterns.
A high or low score is not a legal conclusion or an individual-case judgment.
